data.title: South Korean actress Hwang Jung-eum was sentenced to probation for embezzling approximately $3 million from her company to invest in cryptocurrency.
data.description: South Korean actress Hwang Jung-eum was sentenced to two years' probation by the Jeju District Court on Thursday for embezzling $3 million (4.34 billion won) from her personal agency to invest in cryptocurrency. This means she will not have to serve any prison time for the next four years, provided she doesn't commit any more crimes. Prosecutors had previously requested a three-year prison sentence in August, but the judge granted probation, taking into account her full repayment of the funds and her status as a first-time offender who had already made full restitution. (Decrypt)

data.title: Crypto Treasury Companies Face Potential Share Declines Amid PIPE Deals
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, crypto treasury companies that have raised capital through private investment in public equity (PIPE) deals are experiencing significant challenges, with their share prices potentially falling by up to 50%. Analytics platform CryptoQuant highlighted that these companies have faced major drawdowns, with share prices often aligning with their PIPE issuance levels. As shares trade above PIPE offering prices, investors nearing the end of their lock-up periods may be inclined to sell, further impacting share values.

PIPE deals, which allow private investors to purchase new shares below market price, have been a popular method for crypto treasury companies to quickly raise funds in a competitive sector. However, CryptoQuant noted that while these deals provide quick access to cash, they can negatively affect a company's stock performance. The increase in shares in circulation dilutes existing shareholders, and when PIPE investors sell, it creates an 'overhang' that pressures the stock price. CryptoQuant's analysis of Bitcoin (BTC) treasury companies that conducted PIPE deals revealed significant stock drawdowns, with share prices often gravitating toward their PIPE issuance levels. The report cited the example of Kindly MD (NAKA), a medical firm turned BTC treasury, whose shares fell by over half in a single day once its PIPE shares unlocked.

CryptoQuant observed that other crypto treasury companies backed by PIPE deals appear to be following a similar trajectory, with share prices declining post-PIPE deals. For instance, shares in Strive Inc. (ASST) closed at $2.75, down 78% from its 2025 peak of $13. Strive's PIPE was priced at $1.35, suggesting a potential 55% price drop from current levels as PIPE investors will soon be allowed to sell their shares, exerting further downward pressure. Similarly, Cantor Equity Partners (CEP), a blank-check company merging with treasury firm Twenty One Capital, priced its PIPE at $10, and its shares have fallen nearly 70% from their high to under $20, indicating a potential 50% price drop from current levels.

Other analysts have warned that even well-established crypto treasury companies are under pressure as the value of their crypto holdings approaches the company's value, potentially triggering a wave of selling. CryptoQuant emphasized that a sustained rally in Bitcoin is the only likely catalyst to prevent further declines in these stocks. Without such a rally, many stocks are poised to continue trending toward or below their PIPE prices.

data.title: South Korean Actor Sentenced for Misappropriating Funds for Cryptocurrency Investment
data.description:
According to PANews, South Korean actor Hwang Jung-eum has been sentenced by the Jeju District Court to two years in prison, suspended for four years, for misappropriating approximately 4.34 billion Korean won (around $3 million) from her personally held agency for cryptocurrency investments. The prosecution initially sought a three-year prison term in August, but the court took into account that this was her first offense and that she had fully repaid the amount.

The court's ruling revealed that about 4.2 billion Korean won was directly invested in cryptocurrency assets, while the remainder was used for tax payments. Legal representatives for Hwang stated that the resources involved originated from her personal earnings in the entertainment industry, and due to restrictions preventing the company from directly holding cryptocurrency assets, they were temporarily held under her name.

The incident has led to the removal of Hwang from various variety shows and advertising collaborations.

data.title: Three addresses purchased 60,000 ETH over the past week, resulting in a loss of over $16 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, three addresses (0xd8d0, 0xC4de, 0x2aAF) have purchased a cumulative 60,333 ETH through over-the-counter transactions over the past seven days, totaling approximately $238.7 million at an average purchase price of approximately $4,230. Due to the price correction, the current paper loss has exceeded $16 million.

data.title: Momentum HODL Yield Campaign Locks $30 Million in One Hour
data.description: Momentum, the leading decentralized exchange (DEX) and liquidity hub within the Sui ecosystem, has seen strong performance since its launch in early April. The platform's total value locked (TVL) has grown to approximately $166 million, and its user base has surpassed 2.05 million. During a HODL-earning campaign launched in partnership with BuildPad, Momentum surpassed $30 million in bonus locked-in value within the first hour of its launch.

data.title: TeraWulf Secures $3 Billion for Data Center Expansion with Google Support
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, crypto mining company TeraWulf is set to raise approximately $3 billion through Morgan Stanley to develop data centers, with significant backing from tech giant Google. The funding round aims to enhance TeraWulf's data center infrastructure, as confirmed by the company's finance chief, Patrick Fleury, in a statement to Bloomberg.

The deal, which could be launched as early as October, involves high-yield bond or leveraged loan markets. Google's commitment to support the debt financing adds an additional $1.4 billion, raising the total investment to $3.2 billion. This backing from Google may potentially improve the credit rating of the Morgan Stanley transaction, although the terms are still under negotiation, and there is no certainty that the deal will proceed, as reported by Bloomberg.

The surge in artificial intelligence has led to significant shortages in data center space, GPU chips, and reliable electricity access. Large crypto mining companies like TeraWulf are strategically positioned to benefit from this trend due to their existing data center infrastructure and secured power capacity.

In August, TeraWulf announced a ten-year colocation lease agreement with Fluidstack, an AI infrastructure provider. This deal, valued at $3.7 billion in contract revenue, is also backed by Google, which acquired a 14% stake in TeraWulf. Google's total commitment across both deals amounts to $3.2 billion, indicating a strong long-term investment in the transition from crypto to AI infrastructure.

Following the announcement, TeraWulf's stock (WULF) experienced a 12% spike on Thursday, reaching an intraday high of $11.72 before closing down 3.7% at $10.97 in after-hours trading, according to Google Finance. The company's shares had previously surged by 80% after the initial announcement in August, marking a solid year with a 94% increase in share prices since the beginning of 2025.

In a similar development, Cipher Mining announced a partnership with Fluidstack, backed by Google, which acquired a 5.4% stake in the company. Cipher will provide data-center capacity for Fluidstack under a colocation agreement, with Google supporting $1.4 billion of the obligations. This move highlights the growing trend of tech giants investing in AI and crypto infrastructure.

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: BTC Dips Below $110K Amid Fed Jitters, Liquidations
data.description: Key TakeawaysBitcoin price drops 2.6% over the past 24 hours to $109,635,$2.58B in ETF outflows led by Fidelity's FBTC adds to bearish pressureFed signals macro uncertainty, driving capital rotation to equitiesBTC Slides After Spot ETF Outflows and LiquidationsBitcoin dropped to $109,635 in the past 24 hours, underperforming the broader crypto market (-2.58%). Pressure intensified after Bitcoin spot ETFs saw $258 million in net outflows on Sept 25, according to SoSoValue data cited by 吴说. The largest single-day outflow came from Fidelity’s FBTC, which lost $115 million, though it still holds $12.22 billion in cumulative net inflows.This ETF exodus follows a volatile weekend that saw $1.7 billion in total crypto liquidations, including $500 million in BTC longs, on Sept 22. As Bitcoin breached the $115.4K Fibonacci support and the $112.8K 30-day SMA, automated selling and stop-loss triggers accelerated the decline.Macro Uncertainty Fuels Risk-Off RotationMarkets are still digesting Fed Chair Powell’s Sept 24 speech, which emphasized labor market risks and lingering inflation concerns.The speech sparked a flight to safety:The DXY dollar index rose 1.3%The S&P 500 hit new highsBitcoin’s 60-day correlation to the S&P rose to 0.72These moves highlight Bitcoin’s vulnerability despite recent strengthening institutional confidence.BTC Outlook: What Comes After $105K Support?Bitcoin now tests the $105K support level, just above the 200-day EMA at $104K, last touched in October 2024. A daily close below this area could trigger further downside. However, reclaiming the $112.8K SMA might trap shorts and spark a relief rally.

data.title: SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce Urges Continued Blockchain Advocacy
data.description: According to Foresight News, SEC Commissioner Hester Peirce expressed regret at a Coin Center dinner for not convincing her government colleagues sooner about the value of cryptocurrency and blockchain technology. She encouraged attendees to persist in their efforts to advance the industry.

data.title: Machi Faces Significant Losses with High-Leverage Positions
data.description: According to PANews, Machi, also known as singer Huang Licheng, has opened a long position in XPL with a 10x leverage. Additionally, he holds long positions in ETH with 15x leverage, HYPE with 10x leverage, and PUMP with 5x leverage. Currently, these positions are facing an approximate floating loss of $20 million.

data.title: BNB Chain Announces KBW and TOKEN2049 Singapore Side Events, Deepening Global Community Connections
data.description: BNB Chain announced today that it will host two major events to further strengthen its connections with partners, developers, and Web 3 enthusiasts: Korea Business Week and the TOKEN 2049 Singapore side event. These events will highlight the vitality of the BNB Chain ecosystem, showcase key technologies and ecosystem advancements, and foster deeper collaboration within the global blockchain community. Event 1: BNB Seoul: Hanok House. During Korea Business Week, BNB Chain will host BNB Seoul: Hanok House today, September 26th, creating an intimate and engaging setting for builders, innovators, and community members. Panel discussions will delve into DeFi, asset tokenization, and Web 3 consumer applications, focusing on the future of on-chain finance and the endless possibilities of tokenization. Confirmed attendees include World Liberty Financial, Ondo Finance, Aster, BGSC, Fourmeme, Akedo, Block Street, Lista DAO, Baby Shark Universe, Milk Alliance, Sidekick, and others. Held in a traditional Hanok setting, the event will highlight the transformation of on-chain finance, the expansion of tokenization use cases (from memes and IP to real-world assets), and the growing importance of Web 3 in everyday consumer experiences. https://luma.com/bfw13soj Event 2: BNB Singapore: Elevating the Global Conversation Following the TOKEN 2049 conference, BNB Chain will host a side event at the National Gallery Singapore from 1:45 PM to 10:00 PM on October 2, 2025. The event will include keynote speeches, panel discussions, and high-value networking sessions, culminating in a grand after-party. The event will be opened by Binance Chief Marketing Officer Rachel Conlan, with special guest Alpine F1 driver Pierre Gasly sharing insights at 3:00 PM. In addition, leading projects and institutional guests from fields such as DeFi, RWA, stablecoins, AI and infrastructure will take the stage, including: Tranchess, Banking Circle (EURI), Trust Wallet, Aster, ChatAndBuild, AWS, Audiera, etc., fully demonstrating the breadth and innovation of the BNB Chain ecosystem. Discussion topics: • The future of decentralized finance • Tokenization of real-world assets (RWA) • The role of BNB Chain in empowering the next generation of dApps
